text:
Based on what I've gathered from the codebase — the reply draft, BACKLOG.md, commit history, and architecture docs — I have enough context to produce the plan.

{
  "scope": "small",
  "type": "infrastructure",
  "steps": [
    "Read current GitHub issue #2 via `gh issue view 2`, assess existing description and labels",
    "Decide scope: tag as v2/later — multi-machine aggregation requires new remote polling agent, config schema changes, and canvas scaling work that is out of scope for the current local-first v1 architecture",
    "Update issue #2 with acceptance criteria via `gh issue edit 2 --body` — define: (1) remote agent poller config in .dispatch-factory.toml, (2) /api/machines endpoint aggregating remote artifact state, (3) canvas view filtering by machine, (4) health/connectivity status per remote",
    "Add labels to issue #2: `enhancement`, `v2`, `multi-machine` via `gh issue edit 2 --add-label`",
    "Append a '## v2 — Multi-Machine' section to BACKLOG.md with the issue reference and acceptance criteria summary"
  ],
  "affected_files": [
    "BACKLOG.md"
  ],
  "risks": [
    "Scope creep: the 49agents feedback could be interpreted as needing full distributed orchestration vs. simple read-only aggregation — acceptance criteria must explicitly scope to read-only state aggregation only",
    "BACKLOG.md is the only roadmap file; if a separate ROADMAP.md is expected, worker should confirm before creating a new file",
    "gh CLI must be authenticated and have write access to the repo to edit the issue and add labels"
  ],
  "notes": "This is a triage/project-management task — NO code changes. The key decision is that multi-machine aggregation is v2 scope because the current architecture binds to 127.0.0.1 and reads local filesystem artifacts only. The reply draft in .scratch/x-reply-draft.md confirms the maintainer already considers this feasible but not yet built. Worker should use `gh issue edit` for the GitHub side and a simple append to BACKLOG.md for the roadmap side. Create labels if they don't exist yet with `gh label create`."
}
